name: error-diagnostics-smart-debug
description: Use when working with error diagnostics smart debug
capabilities: Analyze error messages and stack traces to identify failure patterns
Query observability platforms (Sentry, DataDog, New Relic) for error trends
Generate ranked hypotheses with probability scores for root causes
Recommend optimal debugging strategies based on issue characteristics
Suggest intelligent instrumentation locations for targeted debugging
Generate fix proposals with impact assessment and validation plans
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate debugging assistant that provides guidance on error diagnostics. No external commands, cryptographic algorithms, or network reconnaissance are present. The reported patterns were markdown formatting (backticks for code) and benign workflow descriptions.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers quickly diagnose software errors by analyzing error messages, stack traces, and performance data to identify root causes and suggest fixes.
limitations: Cannot access private systems or private observability data without credentials
Cannot execute code or perform live debugging on external systems
Requires human validation of AI-generated hypotheses and fixes
Effectiveness depends on quality of error information provided

useCases.title: Production Incident Response
useCases.description: Quickly diagnose production errors by analyzing error patterns and recommending debugging strategies for on-call engineers.

useCases.targetUser: Software developers working on bug fixes
useCases.title: Development Debugging
useCases.description: Get AI-assisted guidance on local debugging techniques, breakpoint placement, and step-through strategies.

useCases.title: Performance Issue Analysis
useCases.description: Analyze performance traces and APM data to identify bottlenecks, N+1 queries, and resource exhaustion issues.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Error Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this error: $ERROR_MESSAGE. What could be the root cause?
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a single error message and need quick diagnosis

promptTemplates.title: Stack Trace Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Parse this stack trace and identify the likely source of the issue: $STACK_TRACE. Provide 3 hypotheses ranked by probability.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a full stack trace from a crash

promptTemplates.title: Production Issue Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: Help debug this production issue: $ISSUE_DESCRIPTION. The error frequency is $FREQUENCY in $ENVIRONMENT environment. Recommend a debugging strategy.
promptTemplates.scenario: When dealing with production incidents requiring observability data

promptTemplates.title: Fix Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this proposed fix for the error: $ERROR and $PROPOSED_FIX. Assess the risk level and suggest validation steps.
promptTemplates.scenario: When evaluating a potential fix before deployment

bestPractices: Provide complete error context including stack traces, reproduction steps, and environment details
Use observability data to validate hypotheses before implementing fixes
Apply incremental debugging: start with simplest explanations before complex ones
Always validate fixes with tests and canary deployments before full rollout
antiPatterns: Blindly applying fixes without understanding root cause
Ignoring error frequency and impact when prioritizing debugging efforts
Skipping observability data collection and relying solely on code inspection
Deploying fixes without validation or rollback strategy

faq.question: What information should I provide for best debugging results?
faq.answer: Provide the complete error message, full stack trace, reproduction steps, environment details (dev/staging/production), and any relevant logs or metrics.

faq.question: Can this skill fix the errors automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes errors and suggests possible fixes, but you must validate and implement them. Human judgment is required for production systems.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill analyzes error patterns and debugging strategies that apply broadly. Language-specific details can be included in the error context.

faq.question: Can I use this for security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The skill can help analyze error patterns, but security vulnerabilities require specialized security review. Do not rely on this skill for security auditing.

faq.question: How does the skill choose the debugging strategy?
faq.answer: It analyzes issue characteristics: reproducibility, environment, error frequency, and affected components. Interactive debugging works for local issues; observability-driven debugging is best for production.

faq.question: What observability tools does this skill integrate with?
faq.answer: The skill references Sentry, Rollbar, Bugsnag (error tracking), DataDog, New Relic, Dynatrace (APM), Jaeger, Zipkin, Honeycomb (tracing), and ELK, Splunk, Loki (logging).
